title: Changes in EU Steel Safeguard Measures
bodyHtml:
A review investigation concerning the safeguard measure applied by the European Union on the import of certain steel products has concluded. The notification to the World Trade Organization by the EU states that the measure will be extended until June 30, 2026.

Additionally, exemptions for developing countries have been updated based on 2023 imports, and Türkiye has become subject to the measure under categories 7 and 24.

On the other hand, changes have been made in favor of small countries regarding the access regime to remaining quotas in the last quarter of the quota period for countries with specific quotas.

Finally, the Commission has concluded that adjustments are necessary in the operation of the measure for two product categories (Categories 1 and 16) to ensure the effectiveness of the measure and the protection of traditional trade flows. Within this framework, the maximum limit that can be used by a single country from the remaining quota volume available at the beginning of each quarter has been set at 15%.

Click here for the related announcement.

Category 7: Non-alloy and other alloy quarto plates:

7208 51 20, 7208 51 91, 7208 51 98, 7208 52 91, 7208 90 20, 7208 90 80, 7210 90 30, 7225 40 12, 7225 40 40, 7225 40 60, 7225 99 00

Category 24: Other seamless pipes:

7304 19 10, 7304 19 30, 7304 19 90, 7304 23 00, 7304 29 10, 7304 29 30, 7304 29 90, 7304 31 20, 7304 31 80, 7304 51 81, 7304 51 89, 7304 90 00, 7304 39 50, 7304 39 82, 7304 39 83, 7304 39 8
